title: Project schema and CRUD with tenant scoping

story.i_want_to: create, read, update, list, and archive projects within my tenant, where each project has a name, slug, repo_url, description, tech_stack, status, and metadata
story.so_that: I can track multiple codebases under my tenant, each with its own work breakdown, progress, and agent assignments

rationale: Projects are the primary organizational unit for work tracking. Each project represents a codebase being developed by AI agents. The slug enables URL-friendly references in the CLI. The metadata jsonb field allows extensibility without schema changes. Archiving (soft delete via status change) preserves data for audit trails while removing active projects from default listings.

acceptance_criteria.description: A Loopctl.Projects.Project schema exists with fields: id, tenant_id, name, slug, repo_url, description, tech_stack, status (enum: active, archived), metadata (jsonb), inserted_at, updated_at
The Project schema uses Loopctl.Schema with tenant_id required; slug is unique within a tenant (composite unique index on [tenant_id, slug])
POST /api/v1/projects (role: user) creates a project; requires name and slug, optional repo_url, description, tech_stack, metadata
GET /api/v1/projects (role: agent+) lists all active projects for the current tenant with pagination support (offset/limit query params)
GET /api/v1/projects/:id (role: agent+) returns a single project with epic count and story count aggregates
PATCH /api/v1/projects/:id (role: user) updates project fields; slug cannot be changed after creation
DELETE /api/v1/projects/:id (role: user) archives the project by setting status to :archived; does not delete the database record
All queries are scoped to the current tenant via both application-level WHERE clause and RLS policy
Project count per tenant is limited by the max_projects tenant setting (default 50); creating beyond the limit returns 422
RLS policy is enabled on the projects table enforcing tenant isolation at the database level
Every mutation (create, update, delete) MUST be recorded in the audit log via Ecto.Multi in the same transaction as the entity change.

test_cases.name: Create project with valid attributes
expected_results: Response body has project with id, name, slug, status = "active"
Project exists in database with correct tenant_id

test_cases.name: Reject duplicate slug within same tenant
expected_results: Response status is 409 or 422 with error on slug

test_cases.name: Allow same slug in different tenants
steps: Create project with slug "shared-slug" in tenant_b context
expected_results: Project creation succeeds — slug uniqueness is per-tenant, not global

test_cases.name: List projects returns only current tenant's projects
Archive project changes status to archived
expected_results: Project record still exists in database
Archived project is excluded from GET /api/v1/projects listing by default

test_cases.name: Agent can read but not create projects
expected_results: POST returns 403 (agent role insufficient for user-level endpoint)

test_cases.name: Get project includes epic and story counts
expected_results: Response includes epic_count and story_count aggregates
Counts reflect actual data in the database

test_cases.name: Project count limit is enforced
expected_results: Error message indicates project limit reached

test_cases.name: Mutation creates audit log entry
steps: Perform the mutation via API endpoint
expected_results: Audit log entry created with correct entity_type, entity_id, action, and actor
Audit entry is in the same transaction (no orphaned entity without audit)

technical_notes: Project status enum: Ecto.Enum with values [:active, :archived]
Slug validation: same rules as tenant slug (lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens, 2-63 chars)
The list endpoint should default to status=active unless ?include_archived=true is passed
Epic/story counts in show endpoint can use subqueries or preloads with count aggregates
Consider a Loopctl.Projects context module with: create_project/2, get_project/1, list_projects/1, update_project/2, archive_project/1
The context functions should accept tenant_id as a parameter (not rely on process dictionary) for explicit scoping at the application level
All test modules MUST use `async: true` (e.g., `use Loopctl.DataCase, async: true` or `use LoopctlWeb.ConnCase, async: true`)
All tests using Mox MUST include `setup :verify_on_exit!`
All mocks MUST be defined centrally in `test/support/mocks.ex`, never in individual test files
